task: Review user-provided annotated geometry references to correct Wave70 mask and geometry alignment method
source_context: User supplied two annotated 768x768 RGBA images showing intended face-level scaffold and semantic region masks for the active portrait.
review_findings: The correct workflow must establish a full visible-face scaffold before deriving eye, brow, nose, mouth, jaw, and neck masks.

Image #1 shows a red visible-face contour that excludes the hair mass but defines the forehead, cheek, jaw, and chin envelope; my previous eye-local traces did not anchor against this full-face contour.
Image #1 shows a shared yellow eye band across both eyes and bridge area; my previous single-eye/eyelid traces were too isolated and did not honor a coherent bilateral eye-zone scaffold.

Image #1 shows a brow/forehead separator and nose axis/side guides; future nose, brow, eyelid, and under-eye masks must be constrained by these face-level guide relationships, not by broad debug boxes.
Image #1 shows a mouth/lip plane and polygon that should constrain mouth/lips/teeth/chin boundaries; generated-output stability cannot substitute for this geometry.
Image #2 shows semantic masks as anatomical regions inside the face/neck scaffold, including separate face skin, neck, nose, eyes, brows, lips, and lower-face regions.

The user's references show why current masks can look shifted: subregions were being built from local polygons without first locking the global visible-face contour, hair occlusion boundary, eye band, centerline, and neck/face separation.
correction_rules: Do not derive or promote any face-detail mask from an isolated local crop alone.
First create a face-level coordinate scaffold: visible face contour, hair occlusion boundary, eye band, brow band, nose axis/side guides, mouth plane, chin/jaw boundary, and neck/face separation.
Every eye-family candidate must be checked against the shared eye band and full-face scaffold, not just an eye-only crop.
Every nose/mouth candidate must be checked against the face centerline, nose side guides, philtrum/mouth plane, and lip polygon.
Every face/neck/skin candidate must be checked against the red face contour and separate neck region visible in the user's semantic reference.
Use these images as user intent reference evidence, not as automatic pass masks. All derived geometry still requires row-specific geometry and promotion hard gates.
next_step: Build a source/user-reference-aligned full-face geometry scaffold before deriving additional face-detail masks or promoting the eyelids candidate.
